vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the visual data. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the white rabbit anchor and soil debris.

plant_audit.P1: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
plant_audit.P2: No plant matter detected in black pot.
plant_audit.P3: Only the white rabbit anchor remains; no Pothos foliage present.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, and devoid of vegetation.
biome_observations.debris: White shell-like fragments (likely eggshell) present in the foreground pot.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: No fungal growth or incidental sprouts observed; the biome appears to be in a state of total senescence or dormancy.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Across the 5-day sequence, there is zero evidence of growth, recovery, or change in the biological status of the pots. The environment is static and shows no signs of active botanical life.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered specimens are absent or have suffered total systemic loss.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white, calcified debris (eggshells) in the soil is the only non-registry element noted.
vision_report.narrative_description: As an expert visual ethologist, I have performed a frame-by-frame audit of the provided sequence. The pots are essentially empty vessels containing soil and a single decorative anchor (the white rabbit). There is no evidence of the expected botanical specimens (P1-P4). The environment is static, and the lack of chlorophyll-rich tissue or structural plant growth indicates a complete failure of the indoor botanical system.
